brief.title: Johnson & Johnson
brief.scope: Contributed to the redesign and maintenance of the Johnson & Johnson portal, supporting a healthcare products company's digital presence with a focus on accessibility and responsive design.
brief.problem: The portal needed a redesign and ongoing maintenance that met accessibility and responsive design standards appropriate for a healthcare-focused audience, built on a PHP/Symfony backend.
key_challenges: Delivering an accessible experience appropriate for a healthcare products audience with diverse needs.
Maintaining and redesigning a portal built on a PHP/Symfony backend.
Ensuring responsive design consistency across the site.
key_contributions: Implemented redesigned UI features using AngularJS integrated with a PHP/Symfony backend.
Maintained responsive, accessible layouts using CSS3 and web standards best practices.
Supported unit testing (Mocha, Chai) to validate redesign changes.
Collaborated with backend engineers on MySQL-backed content and data features.
technical_focus: Responsive and accessible design for a healthcare audience.
brief.suggested_diagrams: Redesign rollout flow across portal sections. Frontend-to-Symfony backend integration. Accessibility considerations mapped to key user flows.
system_impact: Delivered a more accessible and responsive experience for a healthcare products portal.
Maintained platform stability while modernizing key sections of the site.
Strengthened frontend-backend collaboration patterns for future maintenance work.
learnings: Designing for a healthcare audience reinforced how critical accessibility and clarity are — the interface itself becomes part of the product's duty of care. This project strengthened my ability to work within existing backend constraints while still delivering meaningful UX improvements.
